macro question is will tariffs tank the
US economy our answers are probably not
after all there are six pillars
currently upholding the resilient US
economy theme we authored in the summer
of 2022 which persists trade does not
constitute a large enough share of US
GDP to up in any of those Dynamics
therefore the resilience of the US
economy is likely to persist moreover
our industry-leading empirical Deep dive
of us business cycle Dynamics continues
to Signal a low probability of
developing recession over a medium-term
Time
that's the good news the bad news is
that tariffs are indeed coming as
evidenced by President Trump confirming
the February 1 start date for 25%
tariffs on imports from Canada and
Mexico the US's two largest trading
partners officials from both economies
hinted retaliation is likely all told
investors should expect us growth to
continue slowing over the medium term
amid elevated policy uncertainty
steepening base effects slowing labor
Supply growth and a likely reduction in
federal expenditures consensus expects
growth to slow so the direction of
travel is unlikely to be a headwind for
asset markets what may emerge as head as
a headwind is persistent negative
economic surprises recall that GDP has
been outperforming consensus estimates
since we authored the resilient US
economy theme in the summer of 2022 our
grd model is now calling for growth to
be reported in line or slightly below
consensus over the next few quarters
that Dynamic is new and may support the
bond market and buy extension risk asset
valuations that is until the five
interconnected hackage Catalyst we
identified begin to materialize this
summer refer to our January 18th Around
